verses.text: A vision of a ram with two horns pushing west, north, and south, none able to stand before him. A rising power. Media-Persia advances unchecked for its appointed season.
A vision of a ram with two horns pushing west, north, and south, none able to stand before him. A rising power. Media-Persia advances unchecked for its appointed season.
A vision of a ram with two horns pushing west, north, and south, none able to stand before him. A rising power. Media-Persia advances unchecked for its appointed season.
A vision of a ram with two horns pushing west, north, and south, none able to stand before him. A rising power. Media-Persia advances unchecked for its appointed season.
A he-goat with a notable horn rushes from the west, breaks the ram, then his great horn snaps and four arise. Sudden conquest. Greece overthrows Persia, then itself divides.
A he-goat with a notable horn rushes from the west, breaks the ram, then his great horn snaps and four arise. Sudden conquest. Greece overthrows Persia, then itself divides.
A he-goat with a notable horn rushes from the west, breaks the ram, then his great horn snaps and four arise. Sudden conquest. Greece overthrows Persia, then itself divides.
A he-goat with a notable horn rushes from the west, breaks the ram, then his great horn snaps and four arise. Sudden conquest. Greece overthrows Persia, then itself divides.
From one a little horn grows great toward the south and the host, casting down the truth, and prospers. Defiant tyranny. A persecutor magnifies himself against God's people.
From one a little horn grows great toward the south and the host, casting down the truth, and prospers. Defiant tyranny. A persecutor magnifies himself against God's people.
From one a little horn grows great toward the south and the host, casting down the truth, and prospers. Defiant tyranny. A persecutor magnifies himself against God's people.
From one a little horn grows great toward the south and the host, casting down the truth, and prospers. Defiant tyranny. A persecutor magnifies himself against God's people.
A saint asks how long, and is told, 'unto two thousand and three hundred days; then shall the sanctuary be cleansed.' A measured trial. God sets the term of His people's affliction.
A saint asks how long, and is told, 'unto two thousand and three hundred days; then shall the sanctuary be cleansed.' A measured trial. God sets the term of His people's affliction.
Daniel seeks meaning, and Gabriel is sent to make him understand; the vision is 'for the time of the end.' Heavenly help. God grants light to those who seek to understand His word.
Daniel seeks meaning, and Gabriel is sent to make him understand; the vision is 'for the time of the end.' Heavenly help. God grants light to those who seek to understand His word.
Daniel seeks meaning, and Gabriel is sent to make him understand; the vision is 'for the time of the end.' Heavenly help. God grants light to those who seek to understand His word.
As Gabriel speaks, Daniel falls into a deep sleep, then is set upright and told 'the last end of the indignation.' Overcome yet raised. The weight of revelation humbles, but God lifts up.
As Gabriel speaks, Daniel falls into a deep sleep, then is set upright and told 'the last end of the indignation.' Overcome yet raised. The weight of revelation humbles, but God lifts up.
The ram is Media and Persia, the goat Greece, the great horn its first king, the four the kingdoms that follow. History named. God foretells the rise and fall of empires.
The ram is Media and Persia, the goat Greece, the great horn its first king, the four the kingdoms that follow. History named. God foretells the rise and fall of empires.
The ram is Media and Persia, the goat Greece, the great horn its first king, the four the kingdoms that follow. History named. God foretells the rise and fall of empires.

In the latter time a fierce king shall arise, mighty 'but not by his own power,' destroying the holy people, yet 'broken without hand.' Doom of the persecutor. God Himself ends the tyrant He permitted.

In the latter time a fierce king shall arise, mighty 'but not by his own power,' destroying the holy people, yet 'broken without hand.' Doom of the persecutor. God Himself ends the tyrant He permitted.

In the latter time a fierce king shall arise, mighty 'but not by his own power,' destroying the holy people, yet 'broken without hand.' Doom of the persecutor. God Himself ends the tyrant He permitted.
The vision is true but for many days; Daniel is left sick and astonished, none understanding it. Burdened by truth. Some of God's revelations weigh heavy on the heart.
